_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-max-thinking)**_

Based on my analysis of the current documentation, here are improvement options organized by category:

---

**Architecture & overview**

- Architecture diagram showing how outbox, Kafka publisher, consumer base, and in-process dispatch fit together
- Component dependency map (which modules depend on which)
- Decision records for key design choices (why outbox pattern, why Kafka-specific vs universal handlers)

**Quick reference materials**

- Quick start page showing minimal "up and running in 5 minutes" example
- API cheat sheet (most-used classes and methods in one page under 80 lines)
- Configuration reference table (all settings, env vars, defaults)
- Topic naming conventions and patterns reference

**Missing operational guides**

- Troubleshooting guide for common failure modes (connection issues, duplicate events, DLQ inspection)
- Deployment guide for running the outbox worker in production
- Monitoring and observability guide (health checks, metrics, logging conventions)
- Migration guide for version upgrades when API changes occur

**Examples and patterns**

- Complete end-to-end example service showing all pieces wired together
- Error handling patterns and retry strategies
- Testing patterns collection (unit, integration, with testcontainers)
- Common anti-patterns to avoid (expanded from current guides)

**Structural improvements**

- Add version changelog documenting API evolution
- Add glossary of domain terms (outbox, DLQ, claim, idempotency)
- Create dedicated "for library consumers" vs "for contributors" documentation paths
- Add table of contents navigation at top of each doc page

**Coverage gaps in existing docs**

- EventEnvelopeFormatter usage examples are missing
- EventBus decorator registration pattern needs complete example
- DispatchHooks configuration and all hook types
- OutboxWorker configuration options and tuning
- Health check endpoint documentation

**Format & organization**

- Split large files that exceed 80 lines into smaller focused documents
- Add consistent cross-references between related guides
- Create code examples as separate runnable files rather than inline snippets
- Add "when to use X vs Y" decision trees for choosing between universal and Kafka-specific implementations

Which of these categories or specific items would you like me to detail further?

---

_**User**_

rate each

---

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-max-thinking)**_

Here's a rating for each item, scored 1-100 based on **impact-to-effort ratio** for a PyPI-distributed library:

---

**Quick reference materials**

| Item | Rating | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Quick start page (minimal "up and running" example) | 92 | Highest value for new adopters. Libraries live or die by first 10 minutes of experience. |
| API cheat sheet (under 80 lines) | 78 | Useful daily reference, low effort to produce. |
| Configuration reference table (all settings, env vars, defaults) | 72 | Prevents support churn. Medium effort to gather all options. |
| Topic naming conventions reference | 45 | Nice to have, but integration-guide.md already covers this briefly. |

**Missing operational guides**

| Item | Rating | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Troubleshooting guide for common failure modes | 88 | High value for production users. DLQ inspection, duplicate events, connection drops are real pain points. |
| Deployment guide for outbox worker in production | 82 | Critical for adoption. Users need to know how to run the worker (systemd, k8s, etc.). |
| Monitoring and observability guide | 65 | Important but can come later. Health checks exist but docs are thin. |
| Migration guide for version upgrades | 35 | Premature at v0.1.0. Add when first breaking change happens. |

**Architecture & overview**

| Item | Rating | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Architecture diagram (components + data flow) | 85 | Visual overview reduces cognitive load. Shows how outbox, publisher, consumer, dispatch fit. |
| Component dependency map | 55 | Useful for contributors, lower value for consumers. |
| Decision records (ADR-style) for key design choices | 62 | Good for maintainers. Overkill for library consumers who just want it to work. |

**Examples and patterns**

| Item | Rating | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Complete end-to-end example service (all pieces wired) | 90 | Single most effective teaching tool. Shows event creation, outbox, worker, consumer, DLQ in one flow. |
| Error handling patterns and retry strategies | 75 | tenacity is a dependency but docs don't show how to tune retries. |
| Testing patterns collection | 68 | Already have tests in repo, but extracting patterns into docs helps adopters. |
| Common anti-patterns (expanded) | 70 | Current guides show some anti-patterns. Worth consolidating into one reference. |

**Structural improvements**

| Item | Rating | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Version changelog | 40 | Low effort, low value at v0.1.0. Revisit at v0.2+. |
| Glossary of domain terms | 58 | "Outbox", "DLQ", "claim", "idempotency" - helpful for junior devs on consuming teams. |
| Separate "for consumers" vs "for contributors" doc paths | 60 | Good for project maturity. Effort is in restructuring, not writing new content. |
| TOC at top of each doc page | 52 | Small quality-of-life improvement. Low effort. |

**Coverage gaps in existing docs**

| Item | Rating | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EventEnvelopeFormatter usage examples | 73 | CloudEvents 1.0 wrapping is a key feature, no usage examples exist today. |
| EventBus decorator registration complete example | 77 | The readme claims it exists but no doc shows how to use it. |
| DispatchHooks configuration and all hook types | 65 | Hooks (dispatch, success, failure, disabled, debug) are documented in readme table but no guide. |
| OutboxWorker configuration options and tuning | 80 | Poll intervals, batch sizes, backoff - production-relevant and undocumented. |
| Health check endpoint documentation | 60 | Linked in readme but no dedicated explanation of what it returns or when it degrades. |

**Format & organization**

| Item | Rating | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Split large files exceeding 80 lines | 70 | consumer-transactions.md is 326 lines. Violates your own doc structure rule. Real but not urgent. |
| Consistent cross-references between guides | 55 | Quality of life. Each guide already links somewhere but links are inconsistent. |
| Runnable code examples as separate files | 65 | Good for CI-able docs. Requires infrastructure beyond sphinx though. |
| Decision trees (universal vs kafka-specific) | 72 | DLQ guide already has a comparison table. Extending this pattern to other choices would help. |

---

**Top 5 by rating:**

1. **Quick start page** (92) - First impression drives adoption
2. **Complete end-to-end example** (90) - Shows everything working together
3. **Troubleshooting guide** (88) - Keeps users from abandoning when things break
4. **Architecture diagram** (85) - Mental model for the whole system
5. **Deployment guide for outbox worker** (82) - Required for actual production use
